title: eriostemon oblongifolius
intro: Eriostemon oblongifolius, also known as the Australian bitter peppermint or golden button, is a small shrub native to eastern Australia, characterized by its yellow or white flowers and distinctive four-angled leaves. The plant is believed to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits associated with Eriostemon oblongifolius include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional Australian medicine. Eriostemon oblongifolius is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy and topical applications.
